task_completion.description: Verify the Sygic GPS Navigation application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the Sygic GPS Navigation interface, identifiable by its map style, search bars, or route planning menus.

task_completion.description: Verify Paris is set as the starting location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route planning interface shows 'Paris' in the starting point field (often at the top of the itinerary list).

task_completion.description: Verify Dijon is added as an intermediate stop or waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route planning interface shows 'Dijon' listed as a stop or waypoint between the start and destination.

task_completion.description: Verify Lyon is set as the final destination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route planning interface shows 'Lyon' in the final destination field (at the bottom of the itinerary list).

task_completion.description: Verify the route order is strictly Paris -> Dijon -> Lyon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The itinerary list visually orders the locations sequentially: Paris first, Dijon second, and Lyon third.

task_completion.description: Verify the route overview or itinerary is visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays the calculated route overview, showing the full path on the map or an itinerary list with estimated travel time (e.g., 'h', 'min') and distance (e.g., 'km').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual Sygic GPS Navigation application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show genuine Sygic GUI elements (menus, map, route planner) rather than a web browser, text editor, or mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the route into a search bar or notes app to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Paris', 'Dijon', and 'Lyon' appear within the structured route planning or itinerary UI elements, not just as a single typed string in a generic text field.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the GUI to plan the route.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show sequential interaction with search fields, 'Add stop' buttons, and route calculation screens rather than jumping instantly to the final state via terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura). A successful route overview screen typically contains at least two of the following UI text strings: 'Route', 'Stop', 'Waypoint', 'km', 'h', 'min'. The standard workflow involves searching for a destination, using an 'Add stop' feature to insert waypoints, and viewing the final multi-waypoint itinerary. Paris, Dijon, and Lyon form a logical north-to-south corridor in France.
